channel.title: Experiences. on Evan Rowbotham
channel.description: Recent content in Experiences. on Evan Rowbotham

item.title: Applied Physics Research
item.description: These past five months, I have been working in The Billinge Group. Broadly, I have been contributing to solving the nanostructure problem, through maintaining and deploying packages (old and new) within our Atomic Structure Analysis Software, Diffpy.

item.title: RL Trading Model
item.description: This is an interesting tidbit. Floating Point Group–a crypto startup–was the first ever internship I got my freshman year of college. I was assigned to the trading team and working on implementing a RL algorithm (inspired by the Almgren-Chriss Model) to reduce slippage.

item.title: LLMs in Computational Social Science
item.description: This summer (2024), I worked at Wharton’s Computational Social Science Lab, specifically under Emily Hu. The CSS Lab – headed by Duncan Watts – is interested in finding ways to operationalize social science in order to provide more solution oriented answers to social problems. In Emily’s group we apply ML, CS, and Network Science models (and principles) to operationalize and better understand team dynamics.

item.title: Optimal Blackjack Policy (RL)
item.description: This was an independent study conducted under Farez Siddiqui, through the Math Department’s Directed Reading Program (DRP).
